subject: 🎯 OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5
body_html:
Also: DeepSeek V4 Lands Without a Single Nvidia Chip  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

April 24, 2026   |   Read online

🎯 OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5

Also: DeepSeek V4 Lands Without a Single Nvidia Chip

 
 
 
 

In partnership with

 Subscribe | Advertise

Welcome, AI enthusiasts

OpenAI introduced GPT-5.5 which brings faster performance and stronger results across coding, reasoning, and research. It’s a clear step toward turning ChatGPT into something much bigger than a chatbot. Let’s dive in!

 

In today’s insights:

  • OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5, aiming Superapp

  • DeepSeek V4 Lands Without a Single Nvidia Chip

  • OpenAI Turns ChatGPT Into a Team of Agents

Read time: 4 minutes

LATEST DEVELOPMENTS

OPENAI
🎯 OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5, aiming Superapp

Introducing GPT-5.5

Evolving AI: GPT-5.5 is a solid upgrade, but the headline underneath it is OpenAI's shift into platform ambitions.

Key Points:

  • GPT-5.5 tops OpenAI's internal benchmarks, but the jump from 5.4 is incremental, not generational.

  • The more revealing signal is the superapp pitch, pulling ChatGPT, Codex, and an AI browser into one enterprise product.

  • OpenAI's release tempo suggests models are becoming features, not products.

Details:

OpenAI released GPT-5.5 on Thursday with better scores across coding, reasoning, and scientific research benchmarks. It is faster and more efficient than GPT-5.4, and available across paid ChatGPT tiers. Brockman described it as a step toward "agentic and intuitive computing" and tied it directly to OpenAI's superapp vision. Chief scientist Jakub Pachocki went further, saying the last two years have been "surprisingly slow." Behind the launch sits a clear strategic pivot: stop selling models, start selling a platform that absorbs enterprise workflows wholesale.

Why It Matters:

AI labs are starting to look like consumer tech companies circa 2012, racing to become the default surface. When models iterate monthly, the moat shifts from intelligence to integration. OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic are now competing for something bigger than accuracy scores. They are competing to be the app you never close.

First impressions of GPT-5.5 from Will Koh

10x the context. Half the time.

Speak your prompts into ChatGPT or Claude and get detailed, paste-ready input that actually gives you useful output. Wispr Flow captures what you'd cut when typing. Free on Mac, Windows, and iPhone.

Try Wispr Flow free

DEEPSEEK
🚀 DeepSeek V4 Lands Without a Single Nvidia Chip

Evolving AI: DeepSeek previewed V4, and for the first time its flagship model was trained end-to-end on Chinese hardware rather than Nvidia GPUs.

Key Points:

  • V4 arrives in Pro and Flash versions, both open source, with a reported one-million-token context window and roughly a trillion parameters.

  • The model was trained on Huawei Ascend chips using Huawei's CANN framework, removing any CUDA dependency from the stack.

  • Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent have already placed bulk orders for Huawei's next-generation AI chips in anticipation of V4-class workloads.

Details:

DeepSeek announced V4 on WeChat with limited pricing and benchmark detail, but the technical outline is striking. The company introduced a new Hybrid Attention Architecture aimed at improving recall over long conversations and pushed the context window to a million tokens. Training reportedly ran on Huawei's Ascend 950PR silicon, the product of months of rewriting core model components to run outside Nvidia's ecosystem. Shares of Chinese chip firms SMIC and Hua Hong jumped on the news, while rival Chinese AI players fell sharply.

Why It Matters:

V4 is less of a market shock than R1 was, because cheap Chinese AI is no longer a surprise. The real signal sits lower in the stack. If a frontier-class model can be trained and served entirely on domestic chips, US export controls stop looking like a ceiling on Chinese AI and start looking like the push that built a parallel hardware ecosystem. How long does Nvidia's moat hold once that stack is proven at scale?

“AI is Going to Fundamentally Change…Everything”

That’s what NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang just said about the AI boom, even calling it “the largest infrastructure buildout in human history.”

NVIDIA’s chips made this real-time revolution possible, but now it’s collaborating with Miso to unlock amazing new advances in robotics.

Already a first-mover in the $1T fast-food industry, Miso’s AI-powered Flippy Fry Station robots have worked 200K+ hours for leading brands like White Castle, just surpassing 5M+ baskets of fried food.

And this latest NVIDIA collaboration unlocks up to 35% faster performance for Miso’s robots, which can cook perfect fried foods 24/7. In an industry experiencing 144% labor turnover, where speed is key, those gains can be game-changing.

There are 100K+ US fast-food locations in desperate need, a $4B/year revenue opportunity for Miso. And you can become an early-stage Miso shareholder today. Hurry to unlock up to 7% bonus stock.

Invest in Miso Today

This is a paid advertisement for Miso Robotics’ Regulation A offering. Please read the offering circular at invest.misorobotics.com.

OPENAI
🤖 OpenAI Turns ChatGPT Into a Team of Agents

Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT

Evolving AI: OpenAI launched workspace agents in ChatGPT, replacing custom GPTs with shared Codex-powered agents that run in the cloud and work across teams.

Key Points:

  • Workspace agents are powered by Codex and run persistently in the cloud, continuing tasks even when users are offline.

  • They can be built once, shared across an organization, and used inside ChatGPT or Slack with admin-controlled permissions.

  • OpenAI is deprecating custom GPTs for business plans, positioning workspace agents as the new foundation for AI at work.

Details:

Teams create an agent by describing a recurring workflow in plain language, then ChatGPT maps the steps, connects tools, and tests the setup. Agents can pull data from Slack, Google Drive, Salesforce, Notion, and more, request approvals, file tickets, and draft reports. OpenAI showed examples like a month-end accounting agent and a weekly metrics reporter. The feature is free in research preview until May 6, 2026, then moves to credit-based pricing for Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Teachers plans.

Why It Matters:

This is OpenAI's clearest move yet away from the single chat window and toward AI as shared infrastructure inside companies. Custom GPTs never became sticky, so OpenAI is betting that permissioned agents with memory, handoffs, and admin controls will. The real test is whether teams trust agents to run work unattended, or keep treating AI as a personal tool.

 👀 Watch tip

This robot beats pro table tennis players by combining vision, prediction, and instant reaction. It shows how AI systems are now closing the loop between thinking and doing, bringing intelligence into real-world motion. Exactly where things start to get interesting.

WATCH: A robot is beating human pros at table tennis

 👀 Click on the image you think is real

QUICK HITS

🧠 Google introduced Workspace Intelligence.

🤖 What is Mythos AI and why could it be a threat to global cybersecurity?

👀 Openai has a GPT‑5.5 Bug Bounty.

🎨 Google Released the DESIGN.md format as opensource.

📈 Trending AI Tools

  • 🎞️ Guideless - AI tool that automatically turns your clicks and workflows into step-by-step video guides*

  • 🎨 Gamma - AI Presentation Maker & Website Builder

  • 📤 Shortwave - Automate your email with AI

  • 🔎 Listen Labs - AI researcher that finds your participants, conducts interviews, and delivers actionable insights

 *partner link
